commit.message:
Fix UTF-8 JNI lookup tests for Android (#1398)

On Android, JNI FindClass throws ClassNotFoundException (not generic
JavaException) and GetMethodID throws NoSuchMethodError. The existing
string-based tests in JniTypeTest.cs already use #if __ANDROID__
conditionals for the same reason — apply the same pattern to the new
UTF-8 overload tests.

Fixes dotnet/android#10993

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Responsive hero images with mobile smart crop

Improve image presentation across breakpoints using aspect-ratio and smart crop:

1. Theme update (image.html):
   - Hero preset mobile: 500x400 → 480x480 (square for smart center-crop)
   - Allows Hugo .Fill() to intelligently crop to center on mobile

2. Layout updates (3 files):
   - activities/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto
   - featured/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto
   - inspirations/single.html: aspect-square md:aspect-auto

Responsive behavior:
- Mobile (<768px): Square format (1:1) with smart center-crop
- Tablet (768px+): Hybrid 3:2 format
- Desktop (1024px+): Cinematic tall hero with overlay text
- All: Preserves aspect ratio, no forced cropping

Build verified: 71 pages generated successfully with 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Minecraft (Steve teaches multiplication) and Frozen (Elsa teaches English)

- Updated video titles to indicate these are real recordings

commit.message:
a11y: Improve WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA compliance with focus & contrast fixes

Accessibility improvements addressing keyboard navigation and color contrast:

1. Icon contrast (WCAG 1.4.11 AA) — Replace low-contrast icon colors:
   - text-gray-400 (4.5:1) → text-gray-700 (10.4:1) in:
     • layouts/partials/activity/booking.html (close icon, calendar icon)
     • layouts/partials/activity/product_info.html (duration, persons icons)
     • layouts/activities/list.html (dropdown arrow icon)

2. Keyboard focus indicators (WCAG 2.4.7 AA):
   - Replace anti-pattern focus:outline-none in activities/li.html
   - Use proper focus-visible pattern: outline-2 outline-offset-2 outline-primary
   - Ensures visible focus when navigating with keyboard

3. Navigation focus (WCAG 2.1.1 A):
   - Add focus-visible patterns to sticky nav links (About, Practical Info, Gallery)
   - Add focus-visible patterns to tag links in activity hero sections
   - Use outline-white for tag links (colored backgrounds)

All changes maintain visual design while improving accessibility compliance.
Build verified: 71 pages generated successfully with 0 errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: Improve code maintainability by reordering inline styles

- Move inline style attributes after class attributes for better readability
- Improves separation of concerns (structure first, styling last)
- Affects 6 layout files: activities/single.html, activities/highlight.html,
  activities/list.html, activities/li.html, tags/list.html, _default/home.html
- All background-image optimizations already in place (WebP, blur, quality reduction)
- Build verified: 76 HTML pages generated successfully, no errors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deploy): run build:esm alongside build in Step 1

The ESM output (dist/esm/) is what the CDN serves, but deploy.sh only
ran `npm run build` (CJS). Any export added to src/index.ts after the
last ESM build would be missing from the CDN-delivered file.

Now both builds run together so dist/esm/ is always in sync before the
artifacts are committed and tagged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: rebuild ESM dist to include PositionManager and missing exports

The dist/esm/index.js was stale — it was not rebuilt after PositionManager,
withObserver, ObserverMixin, log/warn, and related types were added to
src/index.ts. The CDN delivery path (dist/esm/) was therefore missing these
exports, causing a SyntaxError at runtime for any consumer importing
PositionManager from the CDN URL.

Ran: npm run build:esm to regenerate all files under dist/esm/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(Portainer): modular compose files + add .env.essential

Task 1: Reorganize Portainer/ directory
- DELETE old monolithic docker-compose-full.yml (1336 lines) and
  docker-compose-minimal.yml (372 lines)
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-postgres.yml — Portainer-specific version
  that uses the base image directly (no build context) and installs locale
  via entrypoint
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-group-minimal.yml — includes Portainer
  postgres + reuses parent redis & ragflow
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-group-databases.yml — includes Portainer
  postgres + reuses parent redis, qdrant, neo4j, minio
- ADD Portainer/docker-compose-full.yml — full stack using include with
  relative paths; only postgres is Portainer-specific

Task 2: Create .env.essential
- Contains only required variables (secrets, passwords, API keys)
- Template with CHANGE_ME placeholders for all secrets
- Optional SMTP section at the end

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Extract identifier sets into constants

Extract hardcoded identifier string checks into reusable constant sets in parser.ts:
- EXAMPLE_IDENTIFIERS: Set(['test', 'it', 'spec', 'example'])
- DESCRIBE_IDENTIFIERS: Set(['describe', 'context', 'suite'])

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for identifier names
- Easier to maintain and extend with new aliases
- O(1) lookup performance with Set.has()
- More readable and intention-revealing code

This follows the DRY principle and makes future identifier additions straightforward.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Port tracing capabilities to Gluon IrisDeviceCtx API (#473)

Add GluonDeviceTracing device-side aggregate for Gluon kernels, mirroring
the existing Triton DeviceTracing with the same SoA buffer layout and
bounds-checked event recording. IrisDeviceCtx.initialize() gains a
tracing constexpr parameter; when False the tracing code is dead-code
eliminated (zero overhead confirmed via assembly diff). IrisGluon host
class wires up the existing Tracing manager so enable/reset/export work
identically to the Triton path.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update amex_automod_remediated.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update amex_automod_remediated.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Overhaul Post-Processing (#828)

Decouple post-processing from `SearchStrategy` so that search methods
accept an explicit post-processor parameter. This enables callers to
supply custom post-processing logic (reranking, filtering, ID
translation) without defining new strategy types.

## Search trait changes

The `Search` trait now requires `S: SearchStrategy<DP, T>` at the trait
level, and introduces two new generic parameters on the `search` method:

- `O` - the output type written to the buffer (used to be a parameter of
`SearchStrategy`)
- `PP` — the post-processor, bounded by `for<'a>
SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync`
- `OB` — the output buffer, bounded by `SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send +
?Sized`

Previously, `OB` was a trait-level generic and the post-processor was
obtained internally from the strategy. Now both are method-level
generics, giving callers control over each independently.

The output "id" type `O` has been removed from `SearchStrategy`
entirely, which greatly simplifies things.

At the `DiskANNIndex` level, two entry points are provided:

- `search()` — requires `S: DefaultPostProcessor`, creates the
strategy's default processor automatically, then delegates to
`search_with()`. This is the common path.
- `search_with()` — requires only `S: SearchStrategy`, accepts any
compatible `PP`. This is the extension point for custom post-processing.

## DefaultPostProcessor

Strategies opt in to default post-processing by implementing
`DefaultPostProcessor`:

```rust
pub trait DefaultPostProcessor<Provider, T, O>: SearchStrategy<Provider, T> {
    type Processor: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<Self::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync;
    fn default_post_processor(&self) -> Self::Processor;
}
```

The `default_post_processor!` macro covers the common case where the
processor is `Default`-constructible. `DefaultSearchStrategy` is a
convenience alias for `SearchStrategy + DefaultPostProcessor`.

## Start-point filtering

The in-mem code used to rely on a special `Internal` type to customize
post-processing for the inplace delete pipeline.
Now that post-processing is customizable, this workaround is no longer
needed.

Start-point filtering is composed at the type level using the existing
`Pipeline` mechanism:

- `HasDefaultProcessor::Processor = Pipeline<FilterStartPoints,
RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>` — filters start points during regular search
- `InplaceDeleteStrategy::SearchPostProcessor = RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy`
— no start-point filtering during delete re-pruning

## Range search

To respond to moving the output buffer to a method level geneirc, range
search now writes results directly through a `DistanceFiltered` output
buffer wrapper that applies radius and inner-radius filtering inline
during post-processing. This replaces the previous approach of
allocating intermediate `Vec`s and copying results through an
`IdDistance` buffer.

`SearchOutputBuffer` is now implemented for `Vec<Neighbor<I>>`,
providing an unbounded growable buffer suitable for range search and
other cases where the result count is not known in advance.

The `RangeSearchResults` type has been removed now that a
`SearchOutputBuffer` is properly used.

## Inplace Delete
Since the post-processor is now a little decoupled from
`SearchStrategy`, rustc is unable to fully resolve the existing bounds
for the "caching" provider. Try as I might, I could not get anything to
work without adding a `DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type to
`InplaceDeleteStrategy`. At the end of the day, I think this is a
compromise we'll have to live with.

## Migration guide

**If you implement `SearchStrategy`** — minimal changes required. Remove
the `O` parfameter (if needed). Implement `DefaultPostProcessor` with
your current post-processor if you want your strategy to work with
`index.search()`. Use the `default_post_processor!` macro for simple
cases:

```rust
impl DefaultPostProcessor<MyProvider, [f32]> for MyStrategy {
    default_post_processor!(Pipeline<FilterStartPoints, RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>);
}
```

**If you call `index.search()`** — no API change for strategies that
implement `DefaultPostProcessor` (all built-in strategies do).

**If you need custom post-processing** — use `index.search_with()` and
pass your post-processor directly:

```rust
let processor = MyCustomPostProcessor::new(/* ... */);
let stats = index.search_with(params, &strategy, processor, &context, &query, &mut output).await?;
```

**If you implement `Search`** — the method signature has changed. `PP`
and `OB` are now method-level generics, and `processor` is an explicit
parameter:

```rust
fn search<O, PP, OB>(
    self,
    index: &DiskANNIndex<DP>,
    strategy: &S,
    processor: PP,       // new
    context: &DP::Context,
    query: &T,
    output: &mut OB,
) -> impl SendFuture<ANNResult<Self::Output>>
where
    O: Send,
    PP: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync,
    OB: SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send + ?Sized;
```

**If you use range search results** — `RangeSearchOutput` no longer
carries `ids` and `distances` fields. Results are written to the output
buffer passed to `search()`. Use `Vec<Neighbor<u32>>` (or any
`SearchOutputBuffer` impl) to collect them.

**If you implement `InplaceDeleteStrategy`**: Add the
`DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type, ensuring it is the same as the
accessor selected by the `SearchStrategy`. This is redundant
information, but needed to help constrain downstream trait bounds.

---------

repository.name: DiskANN
repository.description: Graph-structured Indices for Scalable, Fast, Fresh and Filtered Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search

commit.message:
Retry on malformed JSON from model instead of failing immediately

Model sometimes produces invalid JSON (unescaped characters, broken
syntax). These are transient — retrying often succeeds. Removed
'parse plan' from the non-retryable error list so JSON parse errors
and missing-JSON errors trigger a retry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(skills): add next-roadmap-phase Copilot skill

Adds a structured skill for proposing and implementing the next library
version milestone. Encodes the propose → confirm → implement workflow
used for v0.13.0-alpha as a reusable, documented process.

- .github/skills/next-roadmap-phase/SKILL.md — full step-by-step skill
- .github/SKILLS.md — registered in Skills Index with trigger phrases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(distance): coordinate range validation + remove deprecated API (v0.13.0-alpha)

- calculateDistance now throws GeoPositionError for out-of-range lat/lon
- Removed deprecated GeoPosition.calculateAccuracyQuality() instance method
- Added 9 new tests covering all validation edge cases (316 total)
- Updated FUNCTIONAL_REQUIREMENTS.md to v0.13.0-alpha with 6 new module sections
- Updated ARCHITECTURE.md versioning table with 0.13.0-alpha milestone
- Updated distance-FRS.md and GeoPosition-FRS.md to reflect changes
- Added CHANGELOG entry for 0.13.0-alpha

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
